title: Fasted Cardio Doesn't Burn More Fat
description: Training on an empty stomach is sold as a fat-loss cheat code. Over a full day the difference disappears, and you may just be lifting worse for it.

title: You Can't Crunch Away Belly Fat
description: Targeted ab work to burn stomach fat is one of fitness's most stubborn myths. The research is clear: you don't get to choose where fat comes off.

title: You Don't Always Need to Squat Ass to Grass
description: Full depth is treated as the only real squat. Depth does matter for some muscles, but the all-or-nothing framing misses what the evidence actually shows.

title: The Anabolic Window Is Hours, Not Minutes
description: The post-workout protein scramble is sold as a 30-minute emergency. The research says the window is open for hours, and total daily protein is what counts.

title: You're Probably Eating More Protein Than You Need
description: The "more is always better" mantra breaks down past a point. The useful ceiling sits lower than most lifters think, and the rest is just expensive.

title: Stretching Won't Save You From Injury
description: Static stretching before training is sold as injury insurance. The evidence says it does little for injury risk and can blunt your strength. Here's what the research actually supports.

title: You Don't Need to Train to Failure
description: Going to absolute failure on every set feels hardcore, but the research shows it adds fatigue without adding much growth. Here's where stopping short actually wins.

title: We Didn't Make the AI Smarter. We Gave It the Answer Key
description: Ask a general-purpose AI about fitness and it confidently repeats the field's oldest myths. Here's how JSON.fit feeds it curated, citation-backed research so it applies the right knowledge instead of guessing.

title: Cardio Isn't Stealing Your Gains
description: The interference effect is real, but "cardio kills gains" is a wild overstatement of it. Here's what the research actually says, and the three things that decide whether cardio costs you anything.

title: If You're Not Sore, It Still Worked
description: Delayed-onset muscle soreness feels like proof your workout worked. The research says it's a signal of novelty, not growth, and chasing it can backfire.

title: Your Deload Is Probably Fake
description: Most lifters either never deload or do a "fake" one that clears no fatigue. Here's what a real deload is, and the honest truth about how thin the evidence for it actually is.

title: The 8-12 Rep Range Isn't Magic
description: The hypertrophy rep range of 8 to 12 reps is one of the most cited rules in fitness. The research says it isn't biologically privileged. Here's what actually drives muscle growth.

title: Why Most Bulks Are Wrecked by Too Much Fiber
description: The standard fiber recommendation scales with calories. For lifters eating 4,000+ kcal, that math gets ugly fast. Here's why bulking and cutting need different fiber strategies.

title: The 30g Protein Rule Is Mostly Made Up
description: You've heard you can only absorb 30 grams of protein per meal. The number is a practitioner extrapolation, not a proven threshold. Here's what the research actually says.

title: The 8-Hour Eating Window Isn't What You Think
description: Time-restricted eating is sold as a magic bullet for fat loss and metabolic health. The longest trial ever run says it's not better than just eating less.

title: Your Rest Timer Is Probably Wrong
description: Most fitness apps default to 60-90 seconds between sets. The research says that's too short for trained lifters. Here's what the evidence actually shows.

title: Why Your Volume Tracking Is Lying to You
description: Most fitness apps either ignore secondary muscle work or count it the same as direct work. Both are wrong. Here's the evidence-based middle ground.

title: Why Your Workout Plan Should Tell You When to Push
description: Most fitness apps give you sets and reps but never tell you how hard to actually train. The research is clear: proximity to failure is the variable that drives muscle growth.

title: JSON.fit vs MyFitnessPal vs MacroFactor vs Hevy
description: An honest comparison of what each app does well, where it falls short, and where JSON.fit fits in.

title: Why You Don't Need Another AI Subscription for Fitness
description: You're already paying for ChatGPT or Claude. Why are fitness apps charging you again for the same thing?

title: How to Create a Personalised Meal Plan with Claude
description: Generate a macro-perfect meal plan, import it, and get grocery lists and meal prep instructions, all for $9.99 once.

title: How to Generate a 12-Week Hypertrophy Program with ChatGPT
description: A step-by-step tutorial walking through the full workflow, from setup to AI prompt to imported program, ready to track.
